title: astragalus alpinus subsp. alpinus
intro: Astragalus alpinus subsp. alpinus, also known as the Alpine Astragalus or Mountain Milk Vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to high-altitude regions in Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic and immune-modulating properties, which help the body to resist stress and disease. The medicinal properties of Astragalus alpinus subsp. alpinus are said to promote overall health and well-being by reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular function, and enhancing immune system function. Astragalus alpinus subsp. alpinus can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to support the body's natural healing processes.
